metadata.title: ZoomInfo Due Diligence - Draft
metadata.subject: Commercial and financial diligence
metadata.author: Deep Research
assumptions: Forecast beyond FY2026 guidance requires assumption: Base case assumes low-single-digit revenue growth in FY2027–FY2028 driven by stabilization in SMB, continued Upmarket expansion, and incremental AI product adoption; margins assumed broadly stable with modest efficiency gains.
Net debt presentation assumes 'net debt' = gross debt (incl. current portion) less cash and restricted cash; TRA liability treated as a separate quasi-debt obligation.
Where only point-in-time metrics are disclosed (e.g., net revenue retention as of year-end), the deck treats the disclosed value as representative of the period-end run-rate.
placeholders: FY2027–FY2028 revenue and margin forecast values (Assumption required; use conservative low-single-digit growth until management provides outlook).
Working capital normalization (Assumption required; subscription billings and unearned revenue dynamics can distort operating working capital).
Standalone vs. take-private cost structure (Assumption required; quantify public company cost removals and PE operating model changes with management).

slides.title: Profit & loss overview
slides.strapline: FY2025 returned to modest growth with resilient non-GAAP margins; GAAP margins are sensitive to restructuring, litigation, and other episodic items.
slides.heading: FY2025 highlights: $1.25B revenue, ~84% gross margin, $446M adjusted operating income; FY2026 guidance implies low growth
body: GAAP revenue increased from $1,214.3m in FY2024 to $1,249.5m in FY2025 (+3% YoY).
Gross profit was $1,049.9m in FY2025 (gross margin ~84% per company disclosure).
Non-GAAP adjusted operating income was $445.9m in FY2025 (36% margin); key add-backs include SBC, amortization of acquired intangibles, restructuring, and litigation settlements.
FY2026 guidance (issued February 9, 2026) calls for revenue of $1.247B–$1.267B and adjusted operating income of $456M–$466M.
slides.noteSource: S1 (FY2025 Form 10-K filed Feb 12, 2026) and S2–S5 (FY2025 quarterly earnings releases filed via Form 8-K).

slides.title: Summary financials
slides.source: S1, S2 (FY2025 Form 10-K and Feb 9, 2026 earnings release).

slides.title: Historical trading analysis
slides.strapline: Revenue growth decelerated post-2022; profitability remains strong on a non-GAAP basis, while GAAP margins reflect episodic items.
body: GAAP revenue increased materially from FY2021 to FY2023, then contracted in FY2024 before returning to growth in FY2025; this pattern is consistent with macro pressure and normalization after earlier growth.
Non-GAAP adjusted operating income remains substantial, but the bridge includes recurring items (e.g., stock-based compensation) and acquisition-related amortization.
Operating cash flow is consistently high relative to GAAP net income, aided by advance billing and subscription contract structure.
Key lens for take-private: stress-test renewal/expansion assumptions vs. net revenue retention in the high-80s to ~90% and assess exposure to AI-driven seat compression.

slides.title: Forecast overview
slides.strapline: Company provides FY2026 guidance; FY2027–FY2028 require conservative assumptions until management provides multi-year outlook.
body: FY2026 guidance (February 9, 2026): revenue $1.247B–$1.267B, adjusted operating income $456M–$466M, and unlevered free cash flow $435M–$465M.
Base case (Assumption required) extends a low-growth profile into FY2027–FY2028 with modest retention improvement driven by Upmarket mix and AI product adoption.
Downside case includes continued seat compression and pricing pressure, holding net revenue retention near ~90% or below and limiting expansion.
Upside case assumes faster monetization of AI-enabled workflows, improved renewal/retention, and incremental international penetration.

slides.title: Key operating metrics
slides.strapline: Retention and Upmarket mix improved through 2025; contracted coverage and deferred revenue support durability, but <100% NRR remains a key risk.

rows: Improved vs FY2024; still below 100%, implying cohort contraction without new logo/expansion offsets.
Upmarket customer count grew; concentration in larger customers can improve durability but can increase deal-size volatility.
RPO provides forward revenue coverage; $887.4m expected to be recognized within one year as of Dec 31, 2025.
International revenue exposure (% of total revenue)
Rest of world revenue was $145.8m in FY2025; business remains predominantly U.S.-centric.

slides.title: Commercial diligence findings
slides.strapline: Key lenses: durability of subscription renewal base, macro sensitivity of SMB, and competitive positioning as AI reshapes GTM workflows.
leftBody: Durability positives: Subscription-heavy model; contracts typically 1–3 years and non-cancelable; meaningful RPO balance supports near-term revenue visibility.
Durability risks: Net revenue retention below 100%; management cites macro pressure and expects NRR to be influenced by Upmarket motion; AI-driven seat compression risk is explicitly disclosed.
rightBody: Competitive dynamics: Company identifies competition from free sources, internal databases, niche providers, and potential entrants including LLM/generative AI players and large ecosystems (CRM/business software).
Commercial opportunity: Continued Upmarket penetration (reported 74% of ACV Upmarket as of Dec 31, 2025) and embedding data/agents into customer workflows could support retention and ARPA.

slides.title: Operational diligence findings
slides.strapline: Operating model combines AI/ML data engine with human-in-the-loop QA; diligence should focus on data sourcing rights, opt-out scalability, and integration resilience.
leftBody: Data engine thesis: Company describes an AI/ML engine gathering billions of data points from millions of sources, supported by research analysts and data scientists for quality assurance and gap-filling.
Data quality / liability: Company discloses a contractual data quality guarantee that may create reimbursement/termination risk if accuracy falls below threshold.
rightBody: Privacy/compliance: Expansion of state privacy laws and public opt-out tooling (e.g., California DROP) may elevate opt-out rates and pressure dataset breadth; multiple privacy-adjacent litigations were disclosed in FY2025 filings.
Ecosystem dependencies: Integrations into CRM and marketing systems are described as critically important; loss of API access by platform operators could disrupt customer workflows and retention.

slides.title: Investment highlights and concerns
slides.strapline: Board-level view of key upside and downside factors for a sponsor-led take-private.
body: Highlights: High gross margin profile and strong cash generation (FY2025 OCF $465m and unlevered FCF $455m); subscription model with meaningful RPO; Upmarket momentum and improving NRR through 2025.
Concerns / red flags: FY2026 guidance implies a low growth ceiling near-term; NRR remains below 100% and disclosures explicitly flag AI-driven seat compression and LLM-driven competitive risk.

Structuring considerations: Large TRA liability (~$2.7B at Dec 31, 2025) and material deferred tax assets subject to realizability judgments; both can impact equity value, cash flow, and diligence priorities.

slides.title: Value creation plan
slides.strapline: Sponsor value creation levers prioritize retention uplift, product-led expansion into workflows, and cost discipline while hardening privacy posture.

columns.heading: Retention & expansion
body: Drive NRR toward 100%+ via Upmarket renewals, seat-value packaging, and customer success playbooks tied to measurable pipeline ROI.

columns.heading: AI monetization
body: Productize AI/agent workflows into priced modules; mitigate seat compression by shifting pricing toward outcomes/usage where feasible.

columns.heading: Margin discipline
body: Sustain non-GAAP operating margin mid-30%s; reduce episodic restructuring by completing real-estate footprint optimization and stabilizing org design.

columns.heading: Privacy & data resilience
body: Industrialize opt-out handling (DROP readiness), strengthen data sourcing rights, and reduce litigation surface area through governance, disclosures, and auditability.

slides.title: Quality of earnings
slides.strapline: Non-GAAP profitability is strong but depends on recurring adjustments; diligence should quantify sustainable run-rate margin and cash conversion.
body: Key recurring adjustments in FY2025 include stock-based compensation ($116m) and amortization of acquired technology/intangibles (~$59m).
Episodic items include restructuring/transaction-related expenses ($40m in FY2025; $102m in FY2024) and litigation settlement charges (notably FY2024).
Cash flow remains robust (OCF $465m in FY2025), supported by advance billing mechanics and unearned revenue; confirm sustainability under slower growth and higher opt-out rates.

slides.title: Summary financials
slides.source: S1–S6 (Forms 10-K / 10-Q / 8-K as cited in source register).

slides.disclaimer: This draft diligence content is prepared for illustrative purposes for a hypothetical sponsor-led take-private scenario. It relies exclusively on publicly available information and may omit material non-public facts. All forward-looking values beyond published company guidance are explicitly labeled as assumptions requiring management confirmation.
